name: Milwaukee (United States)

colors.symbolism: Lake Michigan, the Milwaukee River, and the city's industries
appearing throughout the central design and seal
appearing in the gear, the ear of corn and the central elements
outlines and detailing throughout the design

description: A white field bearing a complex assemblage of allegorical figures and emblems — including a flag-bearing figure, an industrial gear, a ship, a stadium, an ear of corn, and the municipal seal — depicting Milwaukee's industry, agriculture, culture and civic life.
history: Designed by alderman Fred Steffan, who incorporated elements from many of the entries submitted to a 1954 city-wide competition, and adopted by the Milwaukee Common Council in 1955. The flag is widely regarded by vexillologists as one of the worst-designed municipal flags in the United States; multiple redesign campaigns (notably the 'People's Flag of Milwaukee' initiative) have proposed alternatives, but the 1955 flag remains the city's official banner.

features.symbolism: An assemblage of devices — a stylised flag-bearing figure, gear, ship, stadium, ear of corn, and embedded municipal seal — combined into a single composition that attempts to depict every facet of Milwaukee's industry, history and civic life.

features.role: embedded composite design including the Milwaukee city seal
features.symbolism: The flag combines a series of allegorical devices — a stylised figure carrying a flag (the city's civic spirit), an industrial gear (manufacturing), a ship's prow on Lake Michigan, an ear of corn (agricultural commerce), County Stadium (sports), the Milwaukee Auditorium (culture), and the official municipal seal — into one image. The design has been widely criticised by vexillologists as a textbook example of an over-cluttered municipal flag.
